The '''Gungan–Naboo War''' was fought between the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo people]] and the indigenous [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] between [[150 BBY/Legends|150 BBY]] and [[121 BBY]]. In the course of the war, the [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Monarch of Naboo]] was killed, and the monarchy subsequently became an elected position. Even after the war, certain Naboo [[Noble/Legends|noble houses]] attempted to continue the war by enlisting [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to claim [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] sources from the Gungans, though they were unsuccessful.

==History==
Prior to the war, conflict between the Gungans and Naboo was rare, if not nonexistent. It is unknown how the war began, but the then-current dynasty expired at the height of the war (c. [[150 BBY/Legends|150 BBY]]), when the last heir was killed in the fighting. Without a ruler, the Naboo elected a Queen to lead them in the war,<ref name="EGtAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>

In an effort to turn the tide of the war in their favor, a coalition of Naboo noble houses struck a secret deal with an offworld [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] group, though the war was resolved without the need for external intervention.<ref name="DPN">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>

===Naboo mercenary conflict===
At some point between [[135 BBY/Legends|135]] and [[125 BBY]], some two decades after the war, certain royal houses struck another secret deal with mercenaries to aid them against the Gungans. The Gungans fought back and eventually prevailed, though they lost many lives and some of their swamp cities.<ref name="DPN" />

Very little was made public about this conflict in Naboo society, and in the years to come, the reality behind it was unknown except to those with access to a noble family's archives. The mercenary group had been hired by several noble families in an effort to claim [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] sources from the Gungans. The Houses that contributed most to funding the mercenary army were [[House Tapalo]] and [[House Veruna]]. Disgusted by this, [[Cosinga Palpatine's grandfather|the patriarch]] of [[House Palpatine/Legends|House Palpatine]] challenged [[Tapalo|the leader of House Tapalo]] to a [[Duel (short story)|duel]] of honor at the Palpatines' ancestral home of [[Convergence/Legends|Convergence]]. Palpatine died at Tapalo's [[Sword/Legends|blade]].<ref name="DPN" />

===Aftermath===
The conflict ended in [[121 BBY]]. Upon the queen's death, the Naboo abolished the hereditary monarchy—in the years to come, Naboo monarchs would be elected.<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref><ref name="ItWoSWEI">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Episode I]]''</ref>

The mercenary conflict remained a controversial point in Naboo politics: in the election of [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]], information was leaked pertaining to the shady role certain royal houses, then opposed to [[Bon Tapalo/Legends|Bon Tapalo]] and his plans to open Naboo's economy to [[The galaxy/Legends|the greater galaxy]], had allegedly played in the first attempt to recruit mercenaries, discrediting their isolationist stance.<ref name="DPN" />

==Behind the scenes==
There is contradictory information on whether the Gungan–Naboo War ever occurred. One source states that the war occurred between 150 BBY and 121 BBY. The ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' states that there was never a war between the Gungans and Naboo, and [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]] says to [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Nass]] in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] that while the Gungans and the Naboo did not always agree, "our two great societies have always lived in peace."

In [[2012]], the novel [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] mentioned the war. Cosinga Palpatine explains to [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]], in a scene taking place in [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]], that, "some two decades after our own conflict with them, the Gungans found themselves embroiled in a war for survival with a mercenary army." Since that would place the first conflict at c. [[155 BBY]] and the second at c. [[135 BBY/Legends|135 BBY]], it is possible that "the Gungan–Naboo War" refers to the overall period of hostilities.
